entity.summary: Fiat-to-crypto brokerage and crypto-asset service platform founded in 2014, with Polish VASP registration and a Latvian EU entity that received MiCA CASP and PSD2 payment-institution licences on May 12, 2026.
entity.notes: The record remains type cex because of the frozen queue, but Paybis is primarily a brokerage / purchase-and-sale platform rather than a traditional order-book exchange. The origin is broadened from Poland to Poland / Latvia to reflect the current Polish VASP context and the licensed EU entity SIA Paybis Europe in Latvia.

expected.summary: Poland-based centralized exchange launched in 2014 and still operating under the Paybis Exchange brand.

changes.summary: Fiat-to-crypto brokerage and crypto-asset service platform founded in 2014, with Polish VASP registration and a Latvian EU entity that received MiCA CASP and PSD2 payment-institution licences on May 12, 2026.
changes.notes: The record remains type cex because of the frozen queue, but Paybis is primarily a brokerage / purchase-and-sale platform rather than a traditional order-book exchange. The origin is broadened from Poland to Poland / Latvia to reflect the current Polish VASP context and the licensed EU entity SIA Paybis Europe in Latvia.

events.title: Paybis was founded in 2014
events.description: Paybis was founded in 2014 as a platform for buying and selling cryptocurrencies through multiple payment methods.
events.notes: The official source establishes the year but not an exact day, so January 1 is used as a year-level marker.

events.title: SIA Paybis Europe received MiCA and PSD2 licences in Latvia
events.description: Latvijas Banka decided on May 12, 2026 to issue SIA Paybis Europe both a MiCA crypto-asset service provider licence and a payment-institution licence under PSD2.
events.notes: The licences apply to the Latvian EU entity and do not replace separate jurisdiction-specific registrations elsewhere.

evidence.title: About Us
evidence.notes: The official company page states that the founders started Paybis in 2014.

evidence.title: Latvijas Banka is issuing two licences to SIA Paybis Europe
evidence.notes: The regulator states that its Supervision Committee decided on May 12 to issue both licences.

evidence.title: Paybis secures EU licences under MiCA and PSD2
evidence.notes: The company announcement describes the dual authorization and EU scope.

evidence.title: Paybis official platform
evidence.notes: The official domain verifies active fiat-to-crypto and crypto-asset services.
